2. Metadata Management (videos.insert)

All video metadata is defined in a local config.json file. The modules/youtube.py module reads this configuration and passes the values to the videos.insert endpoint via the snippet and status resource parts.

Field in config.jsonYouTube API resource fieldExample value
titlesnippet.title🌍 Afrofuturism Highlife Wave 2026 | Assirem Music PROD
descriptionsnippet.descriptionMulti-line description with timestamps and hashtags
tagssnippet.tags["afrofuturism", "highlife", "ai music", …]
categoryIdsnippet.categoryId10 (Music)
defaultLanguagesnippet.defaultLanguage"en"
defaultAudioLanguagesnippet.defaultAudioLanguage"en"
privacyStatus / scheduled_atstatus.privacyStatus + publishAt"private" or scheduled ISO datetime
madeForKidsstatus.selfDeclaredMadeForKidsfalse

3. Thumbnail Handling (thumbnails.set)

After a successful videos.insert, the pipeline calls thumbnails.set with the returned videoId and a local JPEG file path defined in the track config or generated automatically under output/<slug>/. 

# modules/youtube.py — thumbnail upload
yt.thumbnails().set(
    videoId=video_id,
    media_body=MediaFileUpload(thumb_path, mimetype="image/jpeg"),
).execute()

4. Playlist Management (playlistItems.insert)

After thumbnail upload, the pipeline calls playlistItems.insert to add the video to one or more thematic playlists on the same owner channel. Playlist IDs are resolved at runtime by listing the channel's playlists and matching by name — no hardcoded IDs from third-party channels. 

# modules/youtube.py — add to playlist
yt.playlistItems().insert(
    part="snippet",
    body={
        "snippet": {
            "playlistId": resolved_playlist_id,
            "resourceId": {"kind": "youtube#video", "videoId": video_id},
        }
    },
).execute()
Playlist name (config.json)Scope
🌍 Afrofuturism & AfrobeatsOwner channel only
🎵 Assirem Music PROD — All TracksOwner channel only (catch-all)
🌏 World MusicOwner channel only
… (other thematic playlists)Owner channel only

5. OAuth2 Authentication Flow

The pipeline uses the standard OAuth2 installed-application flow (InstalledAppFlow from google-auth-oauthlib). The refresh token is generated once by authorising with the channel owner account and cached locally in credentials/youtube_oauth.pickle. All subsequent uploads use the cached token — no browser prompt is required.

1
First run: InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file() opens a browser window pointing to Google's consent screen. The user signs in as assirem.music.prod@gmail.com (channel owner).
2
Token caching: Credentials are pickled to credentials/youtube_oauth.pickle. Only this one account is ever authorised — the pipeline has no mechanism to add other accounts.
3
Subsequent runs: The pickle is loaded; if the access token is expired, creds.refresh(Request()) silently renews it using the stored refresh token — no user interaction needed.

6. End-to-End Upload — Terminal Output

The following is representative terminal output from a single upload run, demonstrating the complete flow: config read → auth → insert → thumbnail → playlist.

7. YouTube Studio Verification

After each upload, the resulting video is visible in YouTube Studio under the channel owner account, with all metadata, thumbnail, and playlist assignment matching the values in config.json.

8. Scope of API Usage — Summary

API endpointPurposeFrequencyTarget
videos.insert Upload original AI-generated music video 1–3× per day (manual trigger) Owner channel only
thumbnails.set Attach custom thumbnail to uploaded video 1× per upload Owner channel only
playlistItems.insert Add video to thematic playlist 1–2× per upload Owner channel only
playlists.list Resolve playlist name → ID Once per session (cached) Owner channel only

No other endpoints are used. The client does not read, modify, or delete videos from any channel other than the owner channel. It does not access comments, subscriptions, analytics, or any other YouTube resource type.
